intro: Anisomeris englesingii, also known as the "Lion's Foot," is a medicinal plant native to Central and South America, belonging to the family Capparaceae. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and gastrointestinal issues, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. Its bioactive constituents include saponins, flavonoids, and triterpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal effects. Anisomeris englesingii is typically prepared as a decoction or infusion for oral consumption, and it is also used in topical applications to treat skin conditions and wounds.
